evals.prompt: I want to generate 20 drug-like molecules from scratch using GenMol. No scaffold — just generate novel molecules and rank them by drug-likeness. Use the hosted NVIDIA API. My NGC_API_KEY is already set in my environment.
evals.expected_output: A working Python script that calls the hosted GenMol endpoint with a masked SAFE input for de novo generation, requests 20 molecules with QED scoring, parses the response, and prints the results ranked by score.

assertions.description: Uses the correct hosted endpoint URL
assertions.check: Script contains 'health.api.nvidia.com/v1/biology/nvidia/genmol/generate'

assertions.description: Sets Authorization header with Bearer token from NGC_API_KEY environment variable
assertions.check: Script contains 'Authorization' and 'Bearer' and 'NGC_API_KEY'

assertions.description: Uses a masked SAFE input pattern for de novo generation (no scaffold)
assertions.check: Script payload smiles field contains a masked fragment pattern like [*{n-m}] with no scaffold prefix

assertions.description: Requests at least 20 molecules (may request more to compensate for GenMol's post-generation filtering)
assertions.check: Script sets num_molecules to 20 or higher (e.g. 30) to ensure at least 20 valid molecules are returned

assertions.description: Uses QED scoring for drug-likeness
assertions.check: Script sets scoring to 'QED'

assertions.description: Parses the molecules list from the response and prints scores
assertions.check: Script accesses result['molecules'] or result["molecules"] and prints or displays each molecule's score

evals.prompt: I have a pyrrolidinone scaffold (SMILES: C1CC(=O)NC1) that I want to decorate with new fragments to generate drug-like analogs. Generate 10 molecules using GenMol with QED scoring. Use the hosted API.
evals.expected_output: A Python script that converts the scaffold SMILES to SAFE notation using safe-mol, handles SAFEFragmentationError fallback for simple ring scaffolds, appends a masked fragment token, notes the motif extension two-sided mask pattern, calls the hosted GenMol endpoint, and displays generated molecules sorted by QED score.

assertions.description: Uses the correct hosted endpoint URL
assertions.check: Script contains 'health.api.nvidia.com/v1/biology/nvidia/genmol/generate'

assertions.description: Uses safe-mol package to encode the scaffold or explicitly uses SAFE notation
assertions.check: Script uses safe.encode or sf.encode, handles SAFEFragmentationError by falling back or explaining the limitation, and includes a SAFE-formatted string containing the scaffold

assertions.description: Appends a masked fragment token to the scaffold
assertions.check: Script payload smiles field contains [*{ and }] masking pattern combined with scaffold content

assertions.description: Requests at least 10 molecules (may request more to compensate for filtering, but output shows top 10)
assertions.check: Script sets num_molecules to 10 or higher, and output or comments indicate showing/taking 10 results

assertions.description: temperature and noise parameters are passed as strings, not floats
assertions.check: Script passes temperature and noise as quoted string values (e.g. '1.0' or "1.0"), not as bare floats

assertions.description: Results are sorted by score before display
assertions.check: Script sorts the molecules list by score field before printing or saving

evals.prompt: Help me set up GenMol locally with Docker on my H100 machine. My NGC_API_KEY is set. Once it's running, do a quick de novo generation of 5 molecules.
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step Docker setup commands that use shell env first and optional repo-root .env overrides, require NGC_API_KEY or NVIDIA_API_KEY fallback plus LOCAL_NIM_CACHE, run GenMol with correct flags, health-check it, then send a no-auth generation request to localhost:8000 producing 5 molecules.

assertions.description: Local setup uses the repo env contract and LOCAL_NIM_CACHE
assertions.check: Output sources repo-root .env only if present, supports NVIDIA_API_KEY fallback to NGC_API_KEY, requires LOCAL_NIM_CACHE, and mounts LOCAL_NIM_CACHE into the container

assertions.description: References the correct GenMol container image and version
assertions.check: Output contains 'nvcr.io/nim/nvidia/genmol:1.0.1'

assertions.description: docker run includes --shm-size=2G
assertions.check: Output contains '--shm-size=2G' or '--shm-size 2G'

assertions.description: Sets NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 for single GPU operation
assertions.check: Output contains 'NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES' and '0'

assertions.description: Includes a health check poll before submitting generation
assertions.check: Output contains a health check against localhost:8000/v1/health/ready

assertions.description: Generation request targets the local endpoint
assertions.check: Script or curl command targets 'localhost:8000/generate' or '127.0.0.1:8000/generate'

evals.prompt: I have a hit molecule I want to optimize for lipophilicity (LogP). The SMILES is CC1=CC=C(C=C1)NC(=O)C. Generate 30 unique analogs ranked by LogP using GenMol. Use the hosted API.
evals.expected_output: A Python script that encodes the hit molecule as a SAFE input with a masked fragment for optimization, calls the hosted GenMol endpoint with LogP scoring and unique=True, requests 30 molecules, and displays results sorted by score.

assertions.description: Uses the correct hosted endpoint URL
assertions.check: Script contains 'health.api.nvidia.com/v1/biology/nvidia/genmol/generate'

assertions.description: Uses LogP scoring
assertions.check: Script sets scoring to 'LogP'

assertions.description: Requests unique molecules only
assertions.check: Script sets unique to True or true

assertions.description: Requests 30 molecules
assertions.check: Script sets num_molecules to 30

assertions.description: Constructs a SAFE input from the hit molecule (not raw SMILES)
assertions.check: Script uses safe.encode or sf.encode on the hit SMILES, or includes the hit molecule encoded in SAFE notation with a masked fragment

assertions.description: Output molecules are ranked by score in descending order
assertions.check: Script sorts molecules by score in descending order (highest score first)
